perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or doc uments of which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be promptly re turned within five (5) business days, accompanied by copies of such documentation, after the evaluatio n set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement has been concluded.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be returned when requested by the Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: This removes the specific timeframe of 'five (5) business days' after the evaluation, and the clause about accompanying copies. This creates ambiguity and makes enforcement uncertain. This change creates an in-text contradiction when read with Section 8, as it becomes unclear how the return of materials interacts with the survival of confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Term. The foregoing commitments shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties, and shall continue for a peri od of five (5) years following the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a return timeframe of 'five (5) business days' and requires copies. The changed text removes the timeframe and the requirement for copies, stating the materials will be returned 'when requested by the Discloser.' Section 8 states that confidentiality obligations continue for five years following the agreement. The changed text removes the defined return timeframe, making it unclear how long the Recipient can hold the Discloser's materials, which directly impacts the enforceability and interpretation of the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 8. This change creates an in-text contradiction because it introduces ambiguity regarding the duration of material possession and confidentiality, which is essential for interpreting the original contract. This satisfies the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Inform ation to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of R ecipient who are required to have the information in order to carry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Recipient has had or will have directors, officers, employees, cons ultants and agents of Recipient to whom Confidential Information is disclosed or who have access to Confidential Information sign a Non- Disclosure Agreement in content substantially simi lar to this Agreement and will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the names of each su ch person who has signed such agreements after such agreements are signed. Recipient agrees th at it will take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have any such information, which measures shall include the highest degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confiden tial Information of a similar nature. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information which may come to its attention.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information which may come to its attention.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to carry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Recipient has had or will have directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient to whom Confidential Information is disclosed or who have access to Confidential Information sign a Non- Disclosure Agreement in content substantially simi lar to this Agreement and will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the names of each su ch person who has signed such agreements after such agreements are signed.' was removed, this creates a strict non-disclosure, conflicting with the implied permissibility in the original section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information which may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the exception allowing disclosure to certain individuals, creating a strict non-disclosure agreement. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, however, considering the Perturbation Explanation and the Original Text, this creates a direct contradiction, as the original text allows for disclosure to specific parties, while the changed text forbids it. This change directly conflicts with the implied permissibility of the original language, creating a clear contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 14. Remedies. Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, R ecipient agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in la w, in equity or otherwise, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. Remedies. Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business. Discloser shall be entitled to seek remedies available at law.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific reference to injunctive relief and acknowledgement that monetary damages are inadequate, the clause now contradicts itself. The initial statement implies strong protection for the Discloser, while the changed text undermines that by suggesting legal remedies are sufficient. The original implies that actual damages do not need to be proven, which is also removed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. Remedies. Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business. Discloser shall be entitled to seek remedies available at law.
perturbation.justification: The original text emphasizes that monetary damages are inadequate and injunctive relief is available without proving actual damages. The changed text removes these specific remedies and only mentions 'remedies available at law.' Since the Contradicted Text and the Changed Text are the same, and the Changed text removes critical details from the original text, and the change contradicts the implications of the original text regarding the available remedies, a strong contradiction exists, as the changed clause implies legal remedies are sufficient, while the original suggested otherwise.
